design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore alien environments, solve environmental puzzles, acquire tools (laser pistol with three modes), overcome enemies and hazards using weapon versatility and environmental awareness
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-hit-death mechanic creates constant tension; player must learn level layouts and enemy patterns through repeated attempts at checkpoints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Survival against impossible odds on a hostile alien world; scientist thrust into combat using acquired alien technology
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately minimalist UI philosophy—no HUD except oxygen bar, no on-screen text, no explicit tutorials or hints. Characters speak unintelligible alien language. Game communicates entirely through cinematic visuals, environmental design, and gameplay feedback. Designer Éric Chahi prioritized visual storytelling and player discovery over guidance systems

difficulty.lives_system: One-hit-death system; any contact with projectiles, creatures, or hazards ends the game immediately
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite checkpoints at specific scene locations; player can restart indefinitely from last checkpoint without penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Numerous checkpoints throughout game. On systems without save functionality (Amiga, older consoles), players receive alphanumeric codes to record checkpoint progress and re-enter later
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High replayability through checkpoint system reduces frustration despite one-hit-death mechanic. Player learns through repetition rather than being locked out. No difficulty settings documented

progression.upgrade_system: Single major upgrade: acquisition of laser pistol from defeated enemy. Pistol features three modes: standard fire, force field generation (blocks enemy fire), and charged shot (breaks through force fields and walls)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Environmental progression through prison complex → cave system → tower structure. Weapon modes unlock implicitly as player discovers their necessity through environmental challenges
replayability_hooks: One-hit-death encourages learning optimal routes and strategies
Laser pistol's three modes create multiple solution pathways to same challenges
Environmental puzzle design rewards observation and experimentation
Checkpoint codes enable speedrun-friendly progression tracking

world_building_techniques: Cinematic opening sequence establishing protagonist (physicist Lester Knight Chaykin) and inciting incident (particle accelerator accident during thunderstorm)
Environmental storytelling through alien architecture and creature design conveying world hostility
Alien companion 'Buddy' provides emotional stakes and cooperative dynamic without dialogue
Climactic sequence shows Lester wounded, rescued by Buddy, escape on dragon-like creature—visual narrative completion
Unintelligible alien language preserves sense of alienation and environmental foreignness
No text UI reinforces immersion and relies on visual communication

boss_design.philosophy: Integrated into narrative climax rather than separated encounter design. Final confrontation with alien captor presents Lester severely wounded but still capable of defeating attacker with Buddy's assistance
notable_bosses: Alien captor/antagonist—final enemy encounter at tower climax; represents both survival challenge and narrative resolution

faction_indicators: Humanoid alien race as antagonists/captors (militaristic prison camp operators)
Buddy (alien companion) as ally figure—suggests oppression of own species by ruling faction
danger_signals: Indigenous alien creatures as environmental threats
Alien soldiers pursuing player with laser weapons
Prison camp setting establishes oppressive regime
Heavily armed humanoid aliens using force field technology indicates military hierarchy
environmental_storytelling: Barren alien planet establishes hostile natural environment
Subterranean prison complex reveals systematic captivity infrastructure
Cave systems suggest escape routes through natural formations
Tower structure serves as final stronghold and narrative climax location
Laser weapon technology indicates advanced civilization with military apparatus
